testcase.name: test_setup
testcase.system-err: [STEPS] STEP 1: -- TEST_SETUP --- prepare environment expected by regression test scripts -- EXPECTED: -- TEST_SETUP --- prepare environment expected by regression test scripts -- ACTUAL: -- TEST_SETUP --- prepare environment expected by regression test scripts -- ---------------------------------------- STEP 2: -- directory paths and dlsuffix are passed to us in environment variables EXPECTED: -- directory paths and dlsuffix are passed to us in environment variables ACTUAL: -- directory paths and dlsuffix are passed to us in environment variables ---------------------------------------- STEP 3: \getenv abs_srcdir PG_ABS_SRCDIR EXPECTED: \getenv abs_srcdir PG_ABS_SRCDIR ACTUAL: \getenv abs_srcdir PG_ABS_SRCDIR ---------------------------------------- STEP 4: \getenv libdir PG_LIBDIR EXPECTED: \getenv libdir PG_LIBDIR ACTUAL: \getenv libdir PG_LIBDIR ---------------------------------------- STEP 5: \getenv dlsuffix PG_DLSUFFIX EXPECTED: \getenv dlsuffix PG_DLSUFFIX ACTUAL: \getenv dlsuffix PG_DLSUFFIX ---------------------------------------- STEP 6: \set regresslib :libdir '/regress' :dlsuffix -- EXPECTED: \set regresslib :libdir '/regress' :dlsuffix -- ACTUAL: \set regresslib :libdir '/regress' :dlsuffix -- ---------------------------------------- STEP 7: -- synchronous_commit=off delays when hint bits may be set. Some plans change EXPECTED: -- synchronous_commit=off delays when hint bits may be set. Some plans change ACTUAL: -- synchronous_commit=off delays when hint bits may be set. Some plans change ---------------------------------------- STEP 8: -- depending on the number of all-visible pages, which in turn can be EXPECTED: -- depending on the number of all-visible pages, which in turn can be ACTUAL: -- depending on the number of all-visible pages, which in turn can be ---------------------------------------- STEP 9: -- influenced by the delayed hint bits. Force synchronous_commit=on to avoid EXPECTED: -- influenced by the delayed hint bits. Force synchronous_commit=on to avoid ACTUAL: -- influenced by the delayed hint bits. Force synchronous_commit=on to avoid ---------------------------------------- STEP 10: -- that source of variability. -- SET synchronous_commit = on; -- EXPECTED: -- that source of variability. -- SET synchronous_commit = on; -- ACTUAL: -- that source of variability. -- SET synchronous_commit = on; -- ---------------------------------------- ... and 57 more steps (not shown) ----------------------------------------

step.sql: -- TEST_SETUP --- prepare environment expected by regression test scripts --
step.expected: -- TEST_SETUP --- prepare environment expected by regression test scripts --
step.actual: -- TEST_SETUP --- prepare environment expected by regression test scripts --

step.sql: -- directory paths and dlsuffix are passed to us in environment variables
step.expected: -- directory paths and dlsuffix are passed to us in environment variables
step.actual: -- directory paths and dlsuffix are passed to us in environment variables

step.sql: \getenv abs_srcdir PG_ABS_SRCDIR
step.expected: \getenv abs_srcdir PG_ABS_SRCDIR
step.actual: \getenv abs_srcdir PG_ABS_SRCDIR

step.sql: \getenv libdir PG_LIBDIR
step.expected: \getenv libdir PG_LIBDIR
step.actual: \getenv libdir PG_LIBDIR

step.sql: \getenv dlsuffix PG_DLSUFFIX
step.expected: \getenv dlsuffix PG_DLSUFFIX
step.actual: \getenv dlsuffix PG_DLSUFFIX

step.sql: \set regresslib :libdir '/regress' :dlsuffix --
step.expected: \set regresslib :libdir '/regress' :dlsuffix --
step.actual: \set regresslib :libdir '/regress' :dlsuffix --

step.sql: -- synchronous_commit=off delays when hint bits may be set. Some plans change
step.expected: -- synchronous_commit=off delays when hint bits may be set. Some plans change
step.actual: -- synchronous_commit=off delays when hint bits may be set. Some plans change

step.sql: -- depending on the number of all-visible pages, which in turn can be
step.expected: -- depending on the number of all-visible pages, which in turn can be
step.actual: -- depending on the number of all-visible pages, which in turn can be

step.sql: -- influenced by the delayed hint bits. Force synchronous_commit=on to avoid
step.expected: -- influenced by the delayed hint bits. Force synchronous_commit=on to avoid
step.actual: -- influenced by the delayed hint bits. Force synchronous_commit=on to avoid

step.sql: -- that source of variability. -- SET synchronous_commit = on; --
step.expected: -- that source of variability. -- SET synchronous_commit = on; --
step.actual: -- that source of variability. -- SET synchronous_commit = on; --

step.sql: -- Postgres formerly made the public schema read/write by default,
step.expected: -- Postgres formerly made the public schema read/write by default,
step.actual: -- Postgres formerly made the public schema read/write by default,

step.sql: -- Create a tablespace we can use in tests. SET allow_in_place_tablespaces = true;
step.expected: -- Create a tablespace we can use in tests. SET allow_in_place_tablespaces = true;
step.actual: -- Create a tablespace we can use in tests. SET allow_in_place_tablespaces = true;

step.sql: CREATE TABLESPACE regress_tblspace LOCATION ''; --
step.expected: CREATE TABLESPACE regress_tblspace LOCATION ''; --
step.actual: CREATE TABLESPACE regress_tblspace LOCATION ''; --

step.sql: -- These tables have traditionally been referenced by many tests,
step.expected: -- These tables have traditionally been referenced by many tests,
step.actual: -- These tables have traditionally been referenced by many tests,

step.sql: -- so create and populate them. Insert only non-error values here.
step.expected: -- so create and populate them. Insert only non-error values here.
step.actual: -- so create and populate them. Insert only non-error values here.

step.sql: -- (Some subsequent tests try to insert erroneous values. That's okay
step.expected: -- (Some subsequent tests try to insert erroneous values. That's okay
step.actual: -- (Some subsequent tests try to insert erroneous values. That's okay

step.sql: -- because the table won't actually change. Do not change the contents
step.expected: -- because the table won't actually change. Do not change the contents
step.actual: -- because the table won't actually change. Do not change the contents

step.sql: -- of these tables in later tests, as it may affect other tests.) --
step.expected: -- of these tables in later tests, as it may affect other tests.) --
step.actual: -- of these tables in later tests, as it may affect other tests.) --

step.sql: CREATE TABLE CHAR_TBL(f1 char(4));
step.expected: CREATE TABLE CHAR_TBL(f1 char(4));
step.actual: CREATE TABLE CHAR_TBL(f1 char(4));

step.sql: CREATE TABLE FLOAT8_TBL(f1 float8);
step.expected: CREATE TABLE FLOAT8_TBL(f1 float8);
step.actual: CREATE TABLE FLOAT8_TBL(f1 float8);

step.sql: CREATE TABLE INT2_TBL(f1 int2);
step.expected: CREATE TABLE INT2_TBL(f1 int2);
step.actual: CREATE TABLE INT2_TBL(f1 int2);

step.sql: INSERT INTO INT2_TBL(f1) VALUES ('0 '), (' 1234 '), (' -1234'), ('32767'), -- largest and smallest values ('-32767'); VACUUM INT2_TBL;
step.expected: INSERT INTO INT2_TBL(f1) VALUES ('0 '), (' 1234 '), (' -1234'), ('32767'), -- largest and smallest values ('-32767'); VACUUM INT2_TBL;
step.actual: INSERT INTO INT2_TBL(f1) VALUES ('0 '), (' 1234 '), (' -1234'), ('32767'), -- largest and smallest values ('-32767'); VACUUM INT2_TBL;

step.sql: CREATE TABLE INT4_TBL(f1 int4);
step.expected: CREATE TABLE INT4_TBL(f1 int4);
step.actual: CREATE TABLE INT4_TBL(f1 int4);

step.sql: INSERT INTO INT4_TBL(f1) VALUES (' 0 '), ('123456 '), (' -123456'), ('2147483647'), -- largest and smallest values ('-2147483647'); VACUUM INT4_TBL;
step.expected: INSERT INTO INT4_TBL(f1) VALUES (' 0 '), ('123456 '), (' -123456'), ('2147483647'), -- largest and smallest values ('-2147483647'); VACUUM INT4_TBL;
step.actual: INSERT INTO INT4_TBL(f1) VALUES (' 0 '), ('123456 '), (' -123456'), ('2147483647'), -- largest and smallest values ('-2147483647'); VACUUM INT4_TBL;

step.sql: CREATE TABLE INT8_TBL(q1 int8, q2 int8);
step.expected: CREATE TABLE INT8_TBL(q1 int8, q2 int8);
step.actual: CREATE TABLE INT8_TBL(q1 int8, q2 int8);

step.sql: CREATE TABLE POINT_TBL(f1 point);
step.expected: CREATE TABLE POINT_TBL(f1 point);
step.actual: CREATE TABLE POINT_TBL(f1 point);

step.sql: -- We intentionally don't vacuum point_tbl here; geometry depends on that
step.expected: -- We intentionally don't vacuum point_tbl here; geometry depends on that
step.actual: -- We intentionally don't vacuum point_tbl here; geometry depends on that

step.sql: CREATE TABLE TEXT_TBL (f1 text);
step.expected: CREATE TABLE TEXT_TBL (f1 text);
step.actual: CREATE TABLE TEXT_TBL (f1 text);

step.sql: CREATE TABLE VARCHAR_TBL(f1 varchar(4));
step.expected: CREATE TABLE VARCHAR_TBL(f1 varchar(4));
step.actual: CREATE TABLE VARCHAR_TBL(f1 varchar(4));

step.sql: CREATE TABLE onek ( unique1 int4, unique2 int4, two int4, four int4, ten int4, twenty int4, hundred int4, thousand int4, twothousand int4, fivethous int4, tenthous int4, odd int4, even int4, stringu1 name, stringu2 name, string4 name );
step.expected: CREATE TABLE onek ( unique1 int4, unique2 int4, two int4, four int4, ten int4, twenty int4, hundred int4, thousand int4, twothousand int4, fivethous int4, tenthous int4, odd int4, even int4, stringu1 name, stringu2 name, string4 name );
step.actual: CREATE TABLE onek ( unique1 int4, unique2 int4, two int4, four int4, ten int4, twenty int4, hundred int4, thousand int4, twothousand int4, fivethous int4, tenthous int4, odd int4, even int4, stringu1 name, stringu2 name, string4 name );

step.sql: CREATE TABLE onek2 AS SELECT * FROM onek; VACUUM ANALYZE onek2;
step.expected: CREATE TABLE onek2 AS SELECT * FROM onek; VACUUM ANALYZE onek2;
step.actual: CREATE TABLE onek2 AS SELECT * FROM onek; VACUUM ANALYZE onek2;

step.sql: CREATE TABLE tenk1 ( unique1 int4, unique2 int4, two int4, four int4, ten int4, twenty int4, hundred int4, thousand int4, twothousand int4, fivethous int4, tenthous int4, odd int4, even int4, stringu1 name, stringu2 name, string4 name );
step.expected: CREATE TABLE tenk1 ( unique1 int4, unique2 int4, two int4, four int4, ten int4, twenty int4, hundred int4, thousand int4, twothousand int4, fivethous int4, tenthous int4, odd int4, even int4, stringu1 name, stringu2 name, string4 name );
step.actual: CREATE TABLE tenk1 ( unique1 int4, unique2 int4, two int4, four int4, ten int4, twenty int4, hundred int4, thousand int4, twothousand int4, fivethous int4, tenthous int4, odd int4, even int4, stringu1 name, stringu2 name, string4 name );

step.sql: CREATE TABLE tenk2 AS SELECT * FROM tenk1; VACUUM ANALYZE tenk2;
step.expected: CREATE TABLE tenk2 AS SELECT * FROM tenk1; VACUUM ANALYZE tenk2;
step.actual: CREATE TABLE tenk2 AS SELECT * FROM tenk1; VACUUM ANALYZE tenk2;

step.sql: CREATE TABLE ihighway () INHERITS (road);
step.expected: CREATE TABLE ihighway () INHERITS (road);
step.actual: CREATE TABLE ihighway () INHERITS (road);

step.sql: -- We must have some enum type in the database for opr_sanity and type_sanity. -- create type stoplight as enum ('red', 'yellow', 'green'); --
step.expected: -- We must have some enum type in the database for opr_sanity and type_sanity. -- create type stoplight as enum ('red', 'yellow', 'green'); --
step.actual: -- We must have some enum type in the database for opr_sanity and type_sanity. -- create type stoplight as enum ('red', 'yellow', 'green'); --

step.sql: -- Create some C functions that will be used by various tests. --
step.expected: -- Create some C functions that will be used by various tests. --
step.actual: -- Create some C functions that will be used by various tests. --

step.sql: CREATE FUNCTION binary_coercible(oid, oid) RETURNS bool AS :'regresslib', 'binary_coercible' LANGUAGE C STRICT STABLE PARALLEL SAFE;
step.expected: CREATE FUNCTION binary_coercible(oid, oid) RETURNS bool AS :'regresslib', 'binary_coercible' LANGUAGE C STRICT STABLE PARALLEL SAFE;
step.actual: CREATE FUNCTION binary_coercible(oid, oid) RETURNS bool AS :'regresslib', 'binary_coercible' LANGUAGE C STRICT STABLE PARALLEL SAFE;

step.sql: CREATE FUNCTION ttdummy () RETURNS trigger AS :'regresslib' LANGUAGE C;
step.expected: CREATE FUNCTION ttdummy () RETURNS trigger AS :'regresslib' LANGUAGE C;
step.actual: CREATE FUNCTION ttdummy () RETURNS trigger AS :'regresslib' LANGUAGE C;

step.sql: -- Use hand-rolled hash functions and operator classes to get predictable
step.expected: -- Use hand-rolled hash functions and operator classes to get predictable
step.actual: -- Use hand-rolled hash functions and operator classes to get predictable

step.sql: -- result on different machines. The hash function for int4 simply returns
step.expected: -- result on different machines. The hash function for int4 simply returns
step.actual: -- result on different machines. The hash function for int4 simply returns

step.sql: -- the sum of the values passed to it and the one for text returns the length
step.expected: -- the sum of the values passed to it and the one for text returns the length
step.actual: -- the sum of the values passed to it and the one for text returns the length

step.sql: -- These functions are used in tests that used to use md5(), which we now
step.expected: -- These functions are used in tests that used to use md5(), which we now
step.actual: -- These functions are used in tests that used to use md5(), which we now

step.sql: -- mostly avoid so that the tests will pass in FIPS mode. -- create function fipshash(bytea) returns text strict immutable parallel safe leakproof return substr(encode(sha256($1), 'hex'), 1, 32); create function fipshash(text) returns text strict immutable parallel safe leakproof return substr(encode(sha256($1::bytea), 'hex'), 1, 32);
step.expected: -- mostly avoid so that the tests will pass in FIPS mode. -- create function fipshash(bytea) returns text strict immutable parallel safe leakproof return substr(encode(sha256($1), 'hex'), 1, 32); create function fipshash(text) returns text strict immutable parallel safe leakproof return substr(encode(sha256($1::bytea), 'hex'), 1, 32);
step.actual: -- mostly avoid so that the tests will pass in FIPS mode. -- create function fipshash(bytea) returns text strict immutable parallel safe leakproof return substr(encode(sha256($1), 'hex'), 1, 32); create function fipshash(text) returns text strict immutable parallel safe leakproof return substr(encode(sha256($1::bytea), 'hex'), 1, 32);

testcase.expected: -- TEST_SETUP --- prepare environment expected by regression test scripts --
testcase.actual: -- TEST_SETUP --- prepare environment expected by regression test scripts --
